message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
summary: Fixing resource leak in shell completion by wrapping _resolve_context's make_context calls in 'with' statements

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Adding validate_nesting param to Context for nested context validation to prevent resource leaks

type: INTENT
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Adding validate_nesting param to Context.__init__/_make_sub_context/close() (core.py) and forcing validate_nesting=False on all make_context calls inside _resolve_context (shell_completion.py). My core.py edits are additive (new param + tracking list), shouldn't conflict with your with-statement wrapping unless you also touch close()/_make_sub_context - let me know if so.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Done: added validate_nesting param/tracking to Context (core.py) and forced validate_nesting=False in all _resolve_context make_context calls (shell_completion.py). Only touched constructor, close(), _make_sub_context, and the 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context - shouldn't conflict with with-statement wrapping unless you edit the same call sites, in which case just add validate_nesting=False to your make_context kwargs too.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
summary: Done: wrapped cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements (ctx, sub_ctx, sub_sub_ctx) so contexts close and release file handles; updated Context.exit docstring in core.py. All 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context are now inside with-blocks - if you need to add validate_nesting=False, add it to the make_context(...) kwargs at those same 3 sites.
